comment_text.sn35.60:1.1: The four kinds of grasping are listed at SN 12.2:6.3.

root_text.sn35.60:1.4: Cakkhuñca paṭicca rūpe ca uppajjati cakkhuviññāṇaṁ. Tiṇṇaṁ saṅgati phasso.
root_text.sn35.60:1.6: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako cakkhusmimpi nibbindati, rūpesupi nibbindati, cakkhuviññāṇepi nibbindati, cakkhusamphassepi nibbindati, vedanāyapi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.60:1.7: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimokkhā ‘pariññātaṁ me upādānan’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.60:1.12: manañca paṭicca dhamme ca uppajjati manoviññāṇaṁ. Tiṇṇaṁ saṅgati phasso.
root_text.sn35.60:1.14: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako manasmimpi nibbindati, dhammesupi nibbindati, manoviññāṇepi nibbindati, manosamphassepi nibbindati, vedanāyapi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.60:1.15: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimokkhā ‘pariññātaṁ me upādānan’ti pajānāti.

translation_text.sn35.60:0.3: The Complete Understanding of All Grasping
translation_text.sn35.60:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you the principle for the complete understanding of all grasping.
translation_text.sn35.60:1.3: And what is the principle for the complete understanding of all grasping?
translation_text.sn35.60:1.4: Eye consciousness arises dependent on the eye and sights. The meeting of the three is contact.
translation_text.sn35.60:1.5: Contact is a requirement for feeling.
translation_text.sn35.60:1.6: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with the eye, sights, eye consciousness, eye contact, and feeling.
translation_text.sn35.60:1.7: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they are liberated, they understand: ‘I have completely understood grasping.’
translation_text.sn35.60:1.8: Ear consciousness arises dependent on the ear and sounds. …
translation_text.sn35.60:1.9: Nose consciousness arises dependent on the nose and smells. …
translation_text.sn35.60:1.10: Tongue consciousness arises dependent on the tongue and tastes. …
translation_text.sn35.60:1.11: Body consciousness arises dependent on the body and touches. …
translation_text.sn35.60:1.12: Mind consciousness arises dependent on the mind and ideas. The meeting of the three is contact.
translation_text.sn35.60:1.13: Contact is a requirement for feeling.
translation_text.sn35.60:1.14: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with the mind, ideas, mind consciousness, mind contact, and feeling.
translation_text.sn35.60:1.15: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they are liberated, they understand: ‘I have completely understood grasping.’
translation_text.sn35.60:1.16: This is the principle for the complete understanding of all grasping.”
